summary:
There is no direct or indirect evidence in the retrieved literature linking optimism to better survival in older adults; while related topics such as depression and mental well-being are discussed, they do not establish a causal or associative relationship specific to optimism. Thus, based only on the provided information, optimism cannot be said to predict superior survival at advanced age.  

$\boxed{\text{B}}$
